provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: This Scheme may be cited as the Public Service (Immigration Service) Scheme, 2006 and shall come into operation on such date as the Minister may, by notice published in the Gazette , appoint.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Scheme shall apply to Immigration employees of Tanzania Zanzibar as well as to Tanzania Mainland.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Scheme, unless the context requires otherwise— “ Act ” means the Public Service Act, 2002; “ ad hoc Committee ” means a committee appointed by the Chief Executive Officer to facilitate appointments of Immigration employees below the rank of Immigration Officer; “ Appellate Authority ” means a body or organ or a person authorized by the Act , to decide upon Immigration employees appeals; " appointment " means recruitment of persons in the Immigration Service and shall include first appointments or appointments on promotions; " Appointing authority " means a body, organ or a person empowered by the Act to make appointments of Immigration employees in the Service; “ approved establishment ” means the establishment of posts in the Immigration Service where there is a provision in the approved budget for their establishment; “ Chairman ” means the Chairman of the Commission and includes a person acting in that office; “ Chief Executive Officer ” means the Director of Immigration Services; “ Commission ” means the Public Service Commission established by the Act ; “ Director ” means the Director of Immigration Services appointed under regulation 4 , and includes any person exercising the powers of the Director duly delegated to him pursuant to section 5 of the Immigration Act , 1995; “ disciplinary authority ” means any person or authority vested with powers under Part IV of this Scheme to take disciplinary measures against any Immigration employee and includes any person to whom those powers have been authorized; [Cap. 54] “ disciplinary offence ” means any act or omission or misconduct or failure to take reasonable care or failure to meet, set targets or failure to discharge duties in a reasonable or satisfactory manner by or on the part of an immigration employee which warrants disciplinary proceedings; " District Immigration Officer " means an immigration officer appointed by the Director to perform such functions conferred upon him with­in the respective district; “ document ” means any document issued under the Immigration Act , 1995, and the Citizenship Act 1995, the Passport and Travel Documents Act , 2002, and their Regulations; [Cap. 54] " employee " means any person employed in the immigration service ; “ employer ” means a person or organization in the Immigration Service, whom Immigration employee entered into a contact of service and is responsible for the payment of salaries of such an Immigration employee ; “ entry and exit points ” means the gazetted place of entry and exit in Tanzania Mainland as well as to Tanzania Zanzibar; " head of unit " means an Immigration Officer appointed by the Assistant Director of Immigration in the respective section to perform such functions within the respective unit ; [Cap. 54] " immigration officer " means any immigration officer of any grade appointed under sub- section (3) of section 4 of the Immigration Act, 1995 and includes the Director; “ leave ” means and include annual leave, maternity leave, paternity leave, sabbatical leave, sick leave, leave pending retirement, leave without pay and convalescent leave; " Minister " means the Minister for the time being responsible for matters relating to immigration; " officer in-charge " means an Immigration Officer appointed by the Director to perform such functions within respective entry and exist point; “ operational service ” means the cadre of supporting staff not employed in the executive or officer grade; " Permanent Secretary " means the Permanent Secretary for the time being responsible for matters relating to Immigration; “ Pensionable Officer ” means a member of the service whose service is pension-able under the Public Service Retirement Benefits Act ; [Cap. 298] “ Public Service Regulations ” means the Public Service Regulatios, 2003, made under section 34 of the Public Service Act 2003; [Cap. 298 ] " Regional Immigration Officer " means an immigration officer appointed by the Director to perform such functions conferred upon him within the respective region; " the Scheme " means the Immigration Service Scheme of 2006; “ section ” means an identified entity within the organizational structure for management purposes; “ service ” means the Immigration service; “ sub accounting officer ” means an officer charged with the duty of accounting for any service in respect of which money have been appropriated by the Immigration Service; " unit " means an identified entity within the organizational structure for specified function; “ warrant holder ” means an authorized officer charged with the duty of holding issues of the Regional Immigration Office; “ warrant officer ” means an authorized officer charged with the duty of accounting for any service in respect of which money have been appropriated within the respective region. Part II – Command and administration of the Service

provisions.title: Appointment of a chief commanding officer
provisions.content: (1) The President shall appoint a chief commanding officer in respcct of the Immigration Service to be known as the Director of Immigration Services. (2) All authorities in the Immigration Service and powers vested upon him under the Scheme shall be exercised on behalf of the President and shall act with obedience abiding to the directives issued in that respect. (3) All authorities, and functions of the Immigration Service shall be performed by immigration employees on behalf of the President.

provisions.title: Powers of the Minister responsible for matters relating to Immigration Service
provisions.content: (1) The Minister shall be leader and overseer in the formulation and implementation of this Scheme and policies governing the Immigration Service. (2) The Minister shall ensure effective implementation of this Scheme in respect of immigration employees within the Immigration service .

provisions.title: Powers of Permanent Secretary responsible for Immigration Service
provisions.content: The Permanent Secretary respeonsible for Immigration Service shall— (a) be the authority in respect of the appointments and confirmation of Immigration employees of the rank of assistant Inspector to the rank of Senior Assistant Commissioner; (b) notify the chief Secretary of vacancies in the post of Director of Immigration Services which the President is the appointing authority; and (c) formulate, promulgate, monitor, evaluate, review and interpret administrative and personnel policies for the Immigrations Service.

provisions.title: Powers of Director of Immigration Services
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Immigration Service shall be the Chief Commanding Officer of the Immigration Service, and shall in that behalf be answerable to the Minister . (2) The Director shall have the authority in respect of the appointment and confirmation in relation to immigration employees below the rank of Assistant Inspector and Operational Services employees. (3) The Director shall be tthe disciplinary authority in relation to Immigration Officers and Operational Service Employees of Immigration Service, and the Director may, subject to the provisions of regulation 49(5) of the Public Service Regulations , 2003, authorize any public servant who is senior to the rank held by the accused officer to exercise such powers, punishment of which cannot warrant dismisal, reduction in rank or reduction in salary. [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (4) The head of section or unit shall have authority in respect of the discipline of employees in the Operational Service under the Immigration Service. (5) The Director shall have the authority to establish an Ad hoc Committee to facilitate the appointment , confirmation and promotion of Immigration employees below the rank of Immigration Officers. (6) The Director shall have the authority of appointing officers to the officer of Region, District, Entry or Exit Points and heads of units or such appointments shall be governed by the overriding principles of selection based on merit, abilities, experience and qualities stipulated in the Scheme of Service. (7) The Director shall have the authority of establishing, upgrading and abolishing entry or exit points, units and sections.

provisions.title: Roles of the Director of Immigration Service
provisions.content: The Director shall have the following roles in respect of Immigration Service— (a) A leader and overseer in the implementation of the policies governing the Immigration Service; (b) to maintain peace and order through Immigration employees by ensuring that they perform their duties efficiently and effectively; (c) to ensure the effective implementation of this Scheme in respect of immigration employees within the Immigration Services; (d) to ensure that all appointments in the Immigration Service are in accordance with the approved establishment ; (e) issue circular and guidelines in relation to immigration and citizenship matters; (f) to ensure the effective implementation of the Identification and Registration of Persons Act of 1986; [Cap. 36] (g) to facilitate the naturalization of aliens; (h) to provide conducive environment for investment and tourism opportunities; (i) to provide conducive working environment for immigration employees to enable them to perform their executive duties efficiently and effectively; (j) to formulate disciplinary authority in respect of all employees of Immigration Service and pensionable common cadre employees; (k) to the sub accounting officer and warrant holder of the Immigration Service; and (l) to appraise the immigration employees, including the operational service employees.

provisions.title: Roles of an Assistant Director of Immigration Service
provisions.content: The Assistant Director of Immigration Service shall have the following roles in respect of the Immigration Service— (a) the Head of Section of Immigration Service; (b) to advise the Director on any matter relating to the section ; and (c) to supervise immigration employees and give directives and ensure proper performance of the Immigration Service activities in his respective section .

provisions.title: Roles of a Regional Immigration Officer
provisions.content: The Regional Immigration Officer shall have the following roles in respect of Immigration Service— (a) the head and representative of the Director within the respective region; (b) to supervise immigration employees and give directives and ensure proper performance of the Immigration Service activities in his respective region; (c) to advice the Regional Commissioner on matters of peace and order in relation to immigration and citizenship; (d) the warrant officer within the respective region; (e) to facilitate performance appraisal of his subordinates within the respective region; and (f) to maintain peace and security within his respective region.

provisions.title: Roles of a District Immigration Officer
provisions.content: The District Immigration Officer shall have the following roles in respect of the Immigration Service— (a) the head and representative of the Regional Immigration Officer within the respective district; (b) to supervise immigration employees and give directives and ensure proper performance of the Immigration Service activities in his respective district; (c) to advise the District Commissioner on matters of peace and order in relation to immigration and citizenship; (d) the warrant officer within the respective region; (e) to facilitate performance appraisal of his subordinates within the respective district; and (f) to maintain peace and security within his respective district.

provisions.title: Role of Officer in-charge of Entry and Exit Points
provisions.content: The Officer in-Charge of Entry or Exit Point shall have the following roles in respect of the Immigration Service— (a) the officer in-charge of Entry or Exit Points; (b) to report directly to the Director of Immigration Service for those in-charge of grade A Entry or Exit points; (c) to report directly to the Regional Immigration Officer for those in-charge of Grade B, C and D Entry or Exit Points; (d) to supervise immigration employees and give directives and ensure proper performance of Immigration Service activities in his respective station; (e) to ensure and maintain peace and order within the Entry or Exit Points for purposes of securing peace to the entire nation; (f) to facilitate and control movement of citizens and non-citizens; and (g) to facilitate performance appraisal of his subordinates under him within the respective station.

provisions.title: Roles of the Head of Unit
provisions.content: The Head of units shall have the following roles in respect of Immigration Service:— (a) the in-charge of the unit ; (b) to supervise immigration employees and give directives and ensure proper performance of the Immigration Service activities in his respective unit ; and (c) to advise the Head of section on any matter relating to the Unit.

provisions.title: Authorization of powers and functions
provisions.content: Subject to the provisions of regulation 49(5) of the Public Service Regulations , 2003, the Director may authorize any officer who is senior to the bank held by the accused public servant, to exercise disciplinary powers punishment of which cannot warrant, in the event the accused person is found guilty, dismissal, reduction in rank or reduction in salary. [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] Part III – The Service

provisions.title: Responsibilities of Immigration, Fire and Rescue Service Department
provisions.content: The Immigration Department, Fire and Rescue Service Department of the Public Service Commission shall be responsible for all matters related to the Immigration Services and shall enable the Public Service Commission to perform its functions provided for in section 10 of the Act by:— (a) monitoring the implementation of decisions made and directives issues by the Public Service Commission in terms of appointments, staff development, ethics and disciplinary issues in the Immigration Services; (b) facilitating merit based recruitment; (c) advising the Secretary to the Public Service Commission on good governance of the Immigration Service; (d) receiving, scrutinizing, and preparing summaries of the Immigration Service appeals and submit them to the Public Service Commission for its decision; and (e) monitoring and evaluating performance within the Immigration Service.

provisions.title: The role of the Immigration Service
provisions.content: (1) The Constitutional and practical role of the Immigration Service is to maintain peace, order, tranquility, harmony, integrity and status in carrying out decision and in administering immigration service for which it is responsible. (2) The Immigration Service is responsible for the facilitation, control and monitoring the movement of the people in and out of the United Republic of Tanzania. (3) The immigration Service is responsible for the implementation of national policies, programmes and decision on immigration matters to secure the growth and development of an efficient and effective system of the Government.

provisions.title: The role of immigration employees to the Government
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration employee shall owes loyalty, dignity and integrity to the Government as well as the Immigration Service which he serves. (2) Every immigration employee shall serve the Immigration Service in accordance with the principles set out in this Scheme recognizing— (a) the accountability of immigration employee to their leaders and to their supervisors; (b) the duty to discharge public function reasonably and according to the law; (c) the duty to conduct himself with impartiality and honest; (d) the duty to adhere to ethical standards and integrity; (e) the duty to serve with high standard of wisdom and integrity; and (f) the duty to comply with the law to uphold the administration of justice.

provisions.title: Terms and conditions of Service
provisions.content: (1) The terms and conditions of the Immigration Service shall be— (a) permanent and pensionable; (b) temporary; and (c) Operational Service. (2) The terns and conditions of the Immigration Service provided for under sub-clause (1) of this clause may be modified by any special terns contained in the agreement contract or letters of appointment of individual immigration employee .

provisions.title: Appointments in the Immigration Service
provisions.content: (1) Appointment in the Immigration Service shall be governed by— (a) the Constitutional of the United Republic of Tanzania, 1977; [Cap. 2] (b) the Public Service Act Cap. 298 of 2002; [ GN No. 168 of 2002 ] (c) the Public Service Regulations , 2003; [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (d) the Immigration Scheme Service, 2006; (e) the Immigration Service Scheme, 2006; (f) the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct, Regulations 65(1) of the Public Service Regulations , 2003; [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (g) Professional Codes of Ethics and Conduct, Regulation ot the Public Service Regulations , 2003; (h) Employment and Labour Relations Act 2004; [ Act No. 6 of 2004 ] (i) the Public Service Retirement Benefit, Act , 1999; (j) the Public Service Management and Employment policy, 1999; [Cap 371] (k) the Security of Employment Act of 1964; [ Cap. 387 ] (l) the Employment and Labour Relations Act , 2004 Act ; [ Cap. 366 ] (m) Standing Orders for the Public Service; (n) Circular No. 1, 2004 of the Public Service; and (o) the Tanzania Immigration Act , 995. [Cap. 54]

provisions.title: Qualification for appointments and promotions
provisions.content: (1) No person shall be appointed to the Immigration Service and promoted unless he meets the following necessary requirements; (a) academic qualifications determined by a certain level of education required in the post concerned as indicated in the relevant scheme of Service; (b) professional competence determined by the skill level of that it profession; (c) age qualification determined by the date of birth shown either in a birth certificate or any other document relevant and legally acceptable; (d) good character and integrity by his appearance, interaction with other people, the manner he conducts himself, express himself and his track record; (e) experience determined by period of time he has served in the same post or equivalent post; (f) citizenship determined by the Tanzania Citizenship Act , 1995; and [Cap. 357] (g) any other qualifications as may be prescribed in that post.

provisions.title: Notification of vacancies
provisions.content: Notification of vacancies shall specify the necessary requirements for the vacant posts and shall follow the procedures provided for in Regulations 7, 11 and 12 of the Public Service Regulations , and administrative directives instructions or orders.

provisions.title: Persons not to be appointed in the Immigration Service
provisions.content: (1) Any person who has been convicted of a criminal offence involving moral turpitude or who has been dismissed from the public Service or expelled from the immigration basic training shall not appointed in the Immigration Service without the prior sanction of the Chief Secretary. (2) Offences involving moral turpitude shall include— (a) an act or behavior that gravely violates the moral sentiment or accepted moral standard of the community; (b) the immorally eulpate quality held to be inherent in some criminal offence as distinguished from other. (3) Any person who is not a citizen of the united Republic of Tanzania shall not be appointed in the Immigration Service. (4) Any person who has been removed or retired in public interest or retired by retrenchment or redundancy from the Service shall not be appointed in the Immigration Service, wihout the prior sanction of the Chief Secretary. (5) Any person who is of unsound mind shall not be appointed in the Immigration Service.

provisions.title: Approved establishment
provisions.content: (1) No appointment may be made in the Immigration Service Approved whether temporarily or otherwise, for which no provision has been made in the estimates. (2) No increase in the number of posts may be made above the authorized establishment without prior approval of the Permanent Secretary (Establishment).

provisions.title: Ad hoc Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Ad hoc Committee shall facilitate appointments and conduct merit based selection of all immigration employees other than those appointed by the President. (2) Members of the Ad hoc Committee shall be from within the Immigration Department, representative from Public Service Commission and shall consist of both women and men. (3) In exercising its functions under sub-clause (1) of this clause the Ad hoc Committee shall— (a) observe the public service management and employment policy, Scheme of Service, guidelines issues by the Public Service Commission, Immigration Act or any appropriate authority; and (b) abide by principles of equality and transparency in appointments and abstain from being influenced by any body outside the Ad hoc committee.

provisions.title: Appointment principles
provisions.content: All appointments in the Immigration Service shall be free from religious belief or political partiality and shall be based on— (a) merit, fairness and open competition; and (b) the prescribed qualifications.

provisions.title: Appointing authority
provisions.content: (1) The ultimate responsibility for appointments of the immigration employees shall rest upon the appointing authorities. (2) The appointing authorities in the Immigration Service shall abide with the principles prescribed under the Act .

provisions.title: Appeal regarding recruitments
provisions.content: (1) Where a candidate is dissatisfied with the way the appointment procedures is conducted to any post which he is interested and which he was a candidate, he shall complain to the appointing authority concerned and if not satisfied with the decision of the appointing authority he shall appeal to the Commission and thereafter to the Chief Secretary. (2) Where a candidate is dissatisfied with the way appointment procedures is conducted may appeal— (a) to the appointing authority within thirty days (30) from the date of notice of selected candidates of employment; (b) if dissatisfied with the decision of the appointing authority he may appeal to the Commission within fourteen days from the date of the decision of the appointing authority; and (c) if dissatisfied with the decision of the Commission may appeal to the Chief Secretary within seven days from the date of the decision of the Commission . (3) The recruitment process shall be fully documented and the document shall be kept for a period of not less than twelve months from the date when such recruitment process ended.

provisions.title: Relationship between immigration employee and his supervisor
provisions.content: (1) All immigration employees shall be loyal to the President of the United Republic of Tanzania and shall perform their duties with regard to the requirements of the Constitution Laws, Regulations, Orders, Directives, and Instructions. (2) All immigration employees shall regard themselves as employees under the same Service and Strive to serve loyally and comply with lawful instructions of their supervisors. (3) Every immigration employee is entitled to recognition and respect for his dignity regardless of hierarchy in the Service. (4) Eveiy immigration employee shall cooperate and respect with his fellow employee at workplace and elsewhere in order to achieve the objective of the Public Service. (5) Subject to the laid down procedures, every immigration employee shall have the right to claim and enquire any issue pertaining to his service , and his employer or supervisor shall grant the right claim or avail him with the appropriate or necessary information required.

provisions.title: Immigration employee accountability
provisions.content: (1) The Accountability in the Service shall be hierachical and immigration employees shall be accountable according to the arrangements from lower to higher ranks and shall base on the chain of command. (2) In the higher rank there shall be the Director of Immigration Services who is the Chief Commanding Officer according to section 5(1)(b)(i) of the Act . (3) Below the Director there shall be Assistant Director who shall be the Head of Section followed by supporting administrative profes­sional and technical staff comprosing of principal, senior, middle and junior level immigration employees. (4) The Director shall be accountable for the functioning of his Institution. (5) Every Head of section shall be accountable for the function of his area of jurisdiction and every immigration employee in charge of any specific area such as Regional Officer, District Officer, Officer in Charge of Entry or Exit Points and Unit shall be accountable for the efficient performance in his area of responsibility. (6) The most senior Immigration Officer is accountable for the actions of all immigration employees working below him. (7) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1), of this cluase every immigration employee shall be accountable for his own per­formance, action, responsibility and proper discharge of his function provided that his senior or any public leader exerts no pressure, undue influence or interference upon him. (8) Every immigration employee who is entrusted with the revenue collection, custody of suspects, record keeping, escort of persons, documents and properties shall be accountable for his reckless ness or negligence.

provisions.title: Termination of appointments
provisions.content: (1) Termination of appointment of an immigration employee shall include— (a) removal or retirement on public interest; (b) retirement on medical grounds; (c) retirement on abolition of office or re-organization of depart­ment; (d) termination by age of retirement; (e) termination upon contesting constitutional leadership or elective political post; (f) retrenchment or redundancy by reason of— (i) abolition of office or post; (ii) re-organization of office; (iii) dissolution of the organization; and (iv) employers incapability to remunerate a public servant; and (g) Termination on disciplinary ground. (2) Procedures for termination of appointments mentioned in paragraph (1) of this scheme shall be as provided in the Public Service Regulations . [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (3) Suggestions for removal or retirement in public interest shall be made by the Director himself in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations , 2003 and shall only be made where the Director is of the opinion that, grounds for such a suggestion do not warrant disciplinary actions. [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ]

provisions.title: Performance contract
provisions.content: Every immigration employee shall sign a performance contract with his supervisor in accodance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations , Provided that the Director shall sign contract with the Permanent Secretary . [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ]

provisions.title: Performance appraisal system
provisions.content: There shall be operated an Open Performance Appraisal and Review System (OPRAS) within the Immigration Service in accordance with the procedures provided for in the Public Service Regulations , Provided that the Director shall be appraised by the Permanent Secretary . [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ]

provisions.title: Training & staff development
provisions.content: (1) The Director shall be responsible for training and development of his staff. (2) Staff development for the purpose of this Scheme, shall be aimed at developing individuals in the skills required for the performance of their present duties or for the performance of future duties. (3) The Director shall prescribe terms and conditions for the immigration employees attendance at various training courses and coordinate the training of all immigration employees in order to avoid unwarranted disparities within the Immigration Service. (4) The Director shall provide adequate provision in the annual budget for funding training programmes.

provisions.title: Communications within the Immigration Service
provisions.content: Communications within the Immigration Service shall be in accordance with the procedures provided in the Public Service Regulations , administrative directives, instructions or orders.

provisions.title: Transport and subsistence allowance
provisions.content: The Immigration employee shall be entitled to— (a) on first appointment , free transport for himself from the College of training to his w orking station; and (b) subsistence allowance when traveling on duty.

provisions.title: The Immigration Service working Documents
provisions.content: The necessary Immigration Service Working Documents shall include— (a) the Constitution of the United Republic of Tanzania; (b) the Constitution of Zanzibar 1984; (c) the Public Service Act , 2002; [Cap. 2] (d) the Public Service Regulations , 2003; [ Act No 8 of 2002 ] (e) the Public Service Retirement Benefits Act , 1999; [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (f) the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct; [Cap. 371] (g) professional Codes of Ethics and Conduct, Regulation, 65(2) of the Public Service Regulations , 2003; (i) the Employment Act ; [Cap. 366] (j) the Security of Employment Act 1964; [Cap. 387] (k) the Employment and Labour Relations Act 2004; [ Act No. 6 of 2004 ; Cap. 47] (l) the National Security Act , 1970; [Cap. 356] (m) Diplomatic Immunity and Privilege Act , 1986; (n) the Identification and Registration of Persons Act , 1986; [ Act No. 11 of 1986 ] (o) the Immigration Act , 1995; (p) the Tanzania Citizenship Act , 1995; [Cap. 54 of 1995] (q) the Immigration Regulations, 1997; (r) the Citizenship Regulations. 1997; (s) the Tanzania Passport and Travel Documents Act , 2002; [ Act No. 20 of 2002 ] (t) the Tanzania Passport and Travel Documents Regulations, 2004; [Cap. 17] (u) the Refugees Act 1998; (v) the Tanzania Investment and Promotion Act 1986; (w) the Zanzibar Investment and Promotion Act 1986; (x) the Standing Orders of the Public Service; and (y) The Public Service Circular.

provisions.title: Intervention of political leaders
provisions.content: (1) An immigration employee shall not seek any political leaders intervention when pursuing matters of his service unless he proves that he has exhausted the executive administrative ladder. (2) Where in any way an immigration employee seeks any political leaders intervention, a political leader whose intervention has been sought, shall before intervening, communicate the matter to the Director. (3) Where the immigration employee pursues any matter concerning his service , he shall do so by starting from his immediate supervisor before going to the higher level of the Immigration Service administration. Provided that he shall not be bound to start from his immediate supervisor where he thinks that justice shall not be done.

provisions.title: Transfers
provisions.content: (1) Where necessary and where it is in the public interest, transfers of immigration employees shall be conducted within the Service for the purposes of improving efficiency of the Service. (2) Where an immigration employee is transferred, he shall be entitled to free transport for himself, spouse and four children or dependants as well as his personal belongings. (3) Where necessary and where it is in the public interest an immigration officer may be transferred to a foreign office representing Tanzania or any other office for the purpose of improving efficiency of the Service.

provisions.title: Leave
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration employee shall be entitled to annual leave which shall be granted by the employer once a year in accordance with the employees leave cycle. (2) Where the immigration employee is denied his annual leave rights by the employer due to exigencies of service , he shall be entitled to one month salary payment in lieu thereof. (3) An immigration officer shall be entitled to annual leave twenty eight of (28) days. (4) There shall be other leave available for the immigration employee in accordance with the provisions of the Public Service Regulations .

provisions.title: Medical service
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration employee serving in whatever terms his spouse, four children or dependants shall be entitled to free medical attention except where such provision is governed by the National Social Security Act , 1997; the National Health Insurance or any other health or medical Scheme. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1) of this clause, every immigration employee shall take all reasonable precautions in accordance with modem health practices for the proper protection of his health.

provisions.title: Compensation for injuries or death
provisions.content: Where the immigration employee sustains injuries or dies in the course of his employment, he shall be entitled to compensation which shall be paid in accordance with the procedures provided in the Public Service Regulations .

provisions.title: Immigration employees representation
provisions.content: The representation of immigration employees in discussion on matters employment welfare in general shall be through the Workers Council and the Joint Staff Council.

provisions.title: Duty to keep records
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of the employer and employee to keep employment records for the purposes of reference for calculation of the employees terminal benefits. (2) Employment records kept by both employer and employee shall be regarded as original copies duly signed by authorized person. (3) Where there is no possibility to get hold of employment records from employers, records in the custody of the employee shall be used for any purpose provided that such records are in the original form.

provisions.title: Grievances handling mechanism
provisions.content: It shall be the role of the Director and every administrative authority at different level to establish a grievances handling mechanism and he shall receive, solve or seek solutions for the grievances of immigration employees under his jurisdiction. Part IV – Discipline

provisions.title: Disciplinary authorities and powers
provisions.content: (1) The disciplinary authority of the Director shall be the Chief Secretary. (2) Subject to the powers and authorities vested upon him by the Chief Secretary, the Director of Immigration Services shall be the disciplinary authority of all immigration officers, pensionable common cadre employees and operational service employees of immigration. Provided that the Director may authorize any public servant who is scni6r to the rank held by the accused public servant, to exercise such powers, punishment of which cannot warrant dismissal, reduction in rank or reduction in salary. (3) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-clause (1) and (2) of this clause, the Chief Secretary shall in relation to any immigration employee whosoever, be the highest ranking disciplinary authority in the Service and may exercise all or any of the powers delegated to a disciplinary authority by this Scheme.

provisions.title: Conduct of immigration
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration employee shall conduct himself with integrity, impartiality and honest and he shall give honest and impartial advice to the President or, as the case may be, to the Minister , Permanent Secretary , Director or his supervisor without fear or favour and shall make all information relevant for decision available to them. (2) Every immigration employee shall not conduct himself in a way which— (a) is illegal; (b) is improper; (c) is unethical; (d) is in breach of the Constitution ; (e) is inconsistent with the Public Service Code of Ethics and Conduct, the Public Service Leadership. Code of ethics and Conduct, Public Service Regulations , the Administrative directives or instructions and Orders; and (f) involves himself in possible maladministration. (3) Every immigration employee shall act within the boundaries of the authority and the responsibilities delegated. (4) Every immigration employee shall not solicit force or accept bribes from a person who he is serving or has already served or will be serving either by doing so in person or by using another person. (5) Every immigration employee shall safeguard public funds and other properties of the public entrusted to him and shall ensure that no damage, loss, or misappropriation occurs to the funds or public property. (6) Every immigration employee shall observe and abide to his senior in discharge of his duties and responsibilities at workplace and else where when performing his duties. (7) Any immigration employee who contravenes the provisions of sub-clause (1), (2), (3), (4), (5) and (6) of this clause, shall be deemed to have committed a disciplinary offence and shall be subject to disciplinary proceedings which shall be conducted in accordance with the procedures provided in the Public Service Regulations . [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ]

provisions.title: Immigration employees integrity and behaviour
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration employee shall serve with high standard and integrity. (2) Every immigration employee shall behave and conduct himself in an ethical manner.

provisions.title: Uniforms
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration officer is eligible to free issues of uniforms. (2) unless otherwise authorized an immigration officer when is on duty shall wear uniform which will be clearly laundered and pressed and in good state of repair, and that uniforms or part of uniform shall not be worn off duty. (3) Every immigration officer shall wear uniform in accordance with the prescribed administrative directives or staff circulars. (4) Every immigration employee shall maintain personal hygiene, dress in respectable attire in accordance with the acceptable nonns of the office as stipulated in staff circulars.

provisions.title: Confidentiality
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration employee shall not without authority disclose official information which has been communicated confidentially within the administration or received confidentially from other government servants. (2) Every immigration employee shall not use any official document or photocopy such letter or any other document or information obtained in the course of discharging his duties for personal ends. (3) Evenimmigration employees shall not communicate with the media on issues related to work or official policy without due permission. (4) Immigration Official information will be released to the media by officials who have been authorized to do so according to the laid down procedures.

provisions.title: Political neutrality and impartiality of immigration employees
provisions.content: (1) Every immigration officer shall have the right to vote in general and civic election and be neutral and impartial while delivering service to the public. (2) Every immigration officer shall not pass information or documents availed through his position in the Service to any political party.

provisions.title: Preliminary investigations for misconduct
provisions.content: (1) Where the disciplinary authority considers necessary to institute disciplinary proceedings against an immigration officer , the disciplinary authority shall make preliminary investigations before instituting disciplinary proceedings. (2) The disciplinary proceedings for the operational service employees shall be as laid down in the Employment and Labour Relations Act , 2004. [ Act No.6 of 2004 ]

provisions.title: Disciplinary proceedings
provisions.content: (1) Where the disciplinary authority decides to institute formal proceedings he shall proceed in accordance with the procedures in Regulations 42, 44, 45, 46, 47 and 48 of the Public Service Regulations , 2003. (2) Where the disciplinary authority decides to institute summary proceedings, he shall proceed in accordance with the procedures in Regulations 43 and 49 of the Public Service Regulations , 2003.

provisions.title: Relieve from duties administrativeiy
provisions.content: Where the disciplinary authority considers that permitting the suspect to continue with his duties and functions, or any particular duty or function with which he is normally charged amounts to commission of the offence or is not in the public interest, he may immediately relieve him of his duties and functions or that particular duty or function pending the outcome of the investigation by police or the disciplinary enquiry. Provided that the disciplinary action under this scheme shall be taken administratively without resorting to interdiction and shall not affect the suspect’s pay.

provisions.title: Punishments
provisions.content: Any immigration officer who commits a disciplinary offence which warrants formal or summary proceedings under this Scheme, shall be awarded appropriate punishment corresponding to the offence provided in the First Schedule of the Public Service Regulations , 2003. [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ]

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) The appellate authorities of immigration employees and the procedures for appeals shall be as provided for in the Public Service Regulations , 2003. [ GN No. 168 of 2003 ] (2) The disciplinary authority and immigration employees shall have the right to appeal if aggrieved with any decision. Part V – Retirement benefits

provisions.title: Age of retirement
provisions.content: (1) The age of retirement for immigration employees shall be as provided for in the retirement benefits law applicable for the time being. (2) Where there is ambiguity on the date of birth of the immigration employee and where there is argument between the employer and the immigration employee as to the accurate date of birth, the date on record disclosed by immigration employee during his first employment, shall be recognized as the accurate date of birth of such an immigration employee . (3) Where only the year has been recorded, and not the actual date of birth of the immigration employee his date of birth shall be deemed to be the first day of July of that year. (4) Where only the month of birth has been recorded, the date of birth shall be deemed to be the 16 th day of that month and the 15 th in the case of February.

provisions.title: Terminal benefits schemes
provisions.content: The terminal benefits schemes available for immigration employees are contributory under the Public Service Pensions Fund for pensionable officers and National Social Security Fund for operational service employees.

provisions.title: Qualifications for terminal benefits
provisions.content: Qualifications for retirement benefits for immigration employees shall be as provided for in the Public Service Retirement Benefits Act , 1999 and the National Social Security Fund Benefit Law. any person or authority vested with powers under Part IV of this Scheme to take disciplinary measures against any Immigration employee and includes any person to whom those powers have been authorized

any act or omission or misconduct or failure to take reasonable care or failure to meet, set targets or failure to discharge duties in a reasonable or satisfactory manner by or on the part of an immigration employee which warrants disciplinary proceedings
